name: AlphaFold 3
tagline: Predict biomolecular structures and their interactions
metaDescription: AlphaFold 3 is Google DeepMind's inference pipeline for predicting the structure of biomolecules and their interactions from an input file.
overview: AlphaFold 3 is Google DeepMind's implementation of the AlphaFold inference pipeline for predicting the structure of biomolecules and their interactions. You give it an input file describing what you want to model, and it returns a predicted structure.

The project runs in two stages. A data pipeline runs on CPU and handles genetic and template searches against a set of biological databases. Inference then runs on a GPU to produce the structure prediction. You can control each stage with the `--run_data_pipeline` and `--run_inference` flags.

The source code is released under the Apache License 2.0, but the trained model parameters are not bundled with it. You must request access from Google through a form, and access is granted at Google DeepMind's sole discretion. You may only use the model parameters if you receive them directly from Google, under the AlphaFold 3 Model Parameters Terms of Use.
features: Implements the AlphaFold inference pipeline for biomolecular structure prediction
Splits work into a CPU data pipeline and GPU-based inference, each toggled with its own flag
Runs through Docker with mounted folders for input, output, model parameters, and databases
Takes a JSON input file that specifies the sequences and parameters to fold
Ships a `fetch_databases.sh` script to download the required genetic databases (including BFD, PDB, MGnify, UniProt, UniRef90, NT, RFam, and RNACentral)
Returns predictions with varying levels of confidence that should be interpreted carefully

gettingStarted.intro: AlphaFold 3 runs through Docker with your input, output, model parameters, and databases mounted as folders. You must first request the model parameters from Google.

steps.heading: Request the model parameters
steps.body: The trained model parameters are not included in the repository. Complete Google DeepMind's request form to ask for access, which is granted at their sole discretion with a response target of 2 to 3 business days. You may only use the parameters if you receive them directly from Google.

steps.heading: Download the genetic databases
steps.body: Run the provided script to fetch the biological databases used by the data pipeline for its genetic and template searches.

steps.heading: Run the pipeline with Docker
steps.body: Start the container with your input, output, model parameters, and databases mounted, then run the inference script on your JSON input file.
steps.code:
docker run -it \
    --volume $HOME/af_input:/root/af_input \
    --volume $HOME/af_output:/root/af_output \
    --volume <MODEL_PARAMETERS_DIR>:/root/models \
    --volume <DATABASES_DIR>:/root/public_databases \
    --gpus all \
    alphafold3 \
    python run_alphafold.py \
    --json_path=/root/af_input/fold_input.json \
    --model_dir=/root/models \
    --output_dir=/root/af_output

useCases: Predict the three-dimensional structure of a protein and its interactions from a sequence input file
Run the CPU data pipeline and GPU inference stages separately to fit available compute
Set up the required genetic databases once and reuse them across many folding jobs
Review the confidence levels attached to each prediction before using it in downstream research
